column39:
Protocol to amend the Framework Agreement on Comprehensive Economic Co-operation and certain Agreements thereunder between the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) and China.
The Protocol provides amendments to the text of the Rules of Origin (ROO) Chapter and introduces a new section on Customs Procedures and Trade Facilitation (CPTF) to the Trade in Goods Agreement. In addition, the Protocol includes a third package of specific commitments under the Agreement on Trade in Services and adds provisions on the promotion and facilitation of investment in the Investment Agreement. Lastly, the Protocol introduces additional rules and areas of coverage for Economic and Technical Cooperation and a Future Work Programme on the further liberalisation of trade in goods, review of product specific rules, and investment liberalisation and protection.

column39: The Second Agreement Concerning Amendment to the CEPA Agreement on Trade in Services eliminates the three-year substantive business operation requirement for most service sectors, providing Macao service providers with easier access to the Mainland market. Additionally, it introduces pilot initiatives in the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area and includes expanded liberalization measures in sectors such as high-tech, financial services, and audio-visual culture. Moreover, it grants professionals in fields like construction and engineering, accounting, medical care, and education facilitative entry conditions into the Mainland of China, empowering Macao service providers to capitalize on their professional strengths and pursue business growth opportunities in the Mainland of China. The Second Amendment Agreement came into effect on the day of signing (i.e. 10 October 2024) and will be implemented on 1 March 2025.

column39:
Notification of the Second Agreement Concerning Amendment to the Agreement on Trade in Services of the Mainland and Hong Kong Closer Economic Partnership Arrangement.

The Second Agreement Concerning Amendment to the Agreement on Trade in Services of the Mainland and Hong Kong Closer Economic Partnership Arrangement (“the Amendment Agreement II”) updates the commitments on liberalisation and facilitation of trade in services under the Agreement on Trade in Services notified to the WTO on 23 March 2016 (WT/REG162/N/1/Add.9 and S/C/N/264/Add.9) and implemented on 1 June 2016 so as to further enhance the level of liberalisation on trade in services under the framework of CEPA.  The Agreement on Trade in Services was first amended on 21 November 2019 as notified to the WTO on 18 March 2020 (S/C/N/264/Add.11 and S/C/N/264/Add.11/Corr.1) to add liberalisation measures which have been implemented since 1 June 2020.  The Amendment Agreement II introduces new liberalisation measures in a number of services sectors, including measures for pilot implementation in the nine Pearl River Delta municipalities in the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area, and includes commitments on domestic regulation for trade in services and facilitation measures on trade and investment, etc.  The Amendment Agreement II came into effect on the day of signing (i.e. 9 October 2024) and will be implemented on 1 March 2025.

column39:
Notification of the entry into force of Decision No. 1 of 2025 of the EFTA-Jordan Joint Committee concerning amendments to Protocol B to the Free Trade Agreement between the EFTA States and the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan concerning the definition of the concept of "originating products" and methods of administrative cooperation, and its Annexes.

Taking into account the adoption on 7 December 2023 by the Joint Committee of the PEM Convention of a new set of modernised rules of origin designed to promote trade between the Contracting Parties to the PEM Convention, the EFTA States and Jordan have agreed to amend Protocol B to the FTA concerning the definition of the concept of "originating products" and methods of administrative cooperation, and its annexes to reflect the latest development and incorporate a dynamic link to the revised PEM Convention.

Key changes include greater flexibility in cumulation of origin, allowing products processed across multiple PEM Contracting Parties to retain their originating status. The new rules also permit duty drawback for most products. In addition, simplified and more flexible product-specific rules are introduced, together with a higher tolerance threshold for non-originating materials. Further flexibility is also provided for accounting segregation. Registered exporters will have the possibility, if agreed between the Parties, to replace certificates of origin with statements on origin, and the issuance of electronic origin certificates is allowed. These updates aim to reduce administrative burdens, simplify trade documentation, and offer businesses additional opportunities within the PEM zone.

To ensure full alignment, as the Bilateral Agreements on Agriculture between the respective EFTA States and Jordan contained a separate set of rules of origin, they were amended separately with a reference to Protocol B on the same date as the adoption of Joint Committee Decision No. 1 of 2025. These revisions ensure that the updated rules of origin and related procedures are consistently applied across the Free Trade Agreement and the Bilateral Agricultural Agreements, thereby maintaining coherence and facilitating smoother trade operations between the Parties.

column39:
Notification of the entry into force of Decision No. 1 of 2021 of the EFTA-North Macedonia Joint Committee concerning amendments to Protocol B concerning the definition of the concept of originating products and methods of administrative cooperation.

The revised rules of the Regional Convention on pan-Euro-Mediterranean preferential rules of origin (PEM Convention) introduce a new set of modernised rules of origin designed to promote trade between the Contracting Parties to the PEM Convention. The EFTA States and North Macedonia have agreed to amend and replace Protocol B to the Free Trade Agreement (FTA) concerning the definition of the concept of “originating products” and Methods of Administrative Cooperation, incorporating a dynamic link to the PEM Convention and applying the revised rules of the PEM Convention on a bilateral basis pending their adoption by the Joint Committee of the PEM Convention.

Key changes include greater flexibility in the cumulation of origin, introducing simplified, more flexible product-specific rules, as well as allowing for the issuance of electronic origin certificates. These updates aim to reduce administrative burdens, simplify trade documentation, and offer businesses more opportunities within the PEM zone.

As Protocol B are referenced in the Bilateral Agreements on Agriculture between the respective EFTA States and North Macedonia, the amendments also necessitated corresponding changes to the bilateral agricultural agreements. These revisions ensure that the updated rules of origin and related procedures are consistently applied across the FTA and the Bilateral Agricultural Agreements, maintaining coherence, and facilitating smoother trade operations between the Parties.

column39:
Notification of the entry into force of Decisions No. 1 – 3 of 2008 of the Joint EFTA-Palestinian Authority Committee concerning amendments to Protocol A concerning processed agricultural products, to Annex I concerning products not covered by the Agreement and to Annex II concerning fish and other marine products.

Decision 1/2008: Taking into account that the EFTA States have revised their agreements with the European Union on processed agricultural products, by expanding the product coverage of these agreements and providing for improved concessions for the products covered, the Decision updates Protocol A to ensure products originating in the West Bank and the Gaza Strip are accorded treatment no less favourable than that accorded to the European Communities. 

Decision 2/2008: Update of Annex I following amendments to Protocol A to the EFTA-Palestinian Authority Agreement given that some products listed in the former Annex I to the Agreement are now included in the Agreement. 

Decision 3/2008: Technical amendments to Table 1 of Annex II to the EFTA-Palestinian Authority Free Trade Agreement to harmonise the scope of the Annex, taking into consideration developments in international trade in fish and other marine products and in particular the discussions taking place in the WTO regarding the definition of fish and fisheries products.
